meta.role: Program Coordinator, Center for Public and Nonprofit Leadership
meta.note: 80-150 word answers in Jamie's voice. Adapted from voice corpus + content library. Workday may not require these; provided in case the questionnaire asks.

answers.question: Why are you interested in this role?
answers.answer: Coordinating programs with a lot of moving parts is genuinely the work I do best. In my current role I manage 20+ educational programs end-to-end—handling event logistics, materials, communications, and the troubleshooting that keeps sessions running on time. This Program Coordinator role is exactly that, focused on executive certificate programs, and I love that side of the work: being the organized, responsive person who makes sure participants and faculty never feel the friction behind the scenes. I'm early in my career, but I pick up new tools quickly and I'm drawn to a role where attention to detail and steady follow-through directly shape whether a program succeeds. That's the impact I'd hope to bring to the Center.

answers.question: Why Georgetown / the Center for Public and Nonprofit Leadership?
answers.answer: What draws me to the Center is its mission—preparing leaders to work for the public good across all sectors. That isn't abstract for me. I volunteer with Global Shapers, an NGO under the World Economic Forum, where I help revamp remote hiring and onboarding, so I care about the nonprofit and public-leadership space and the people building it. I'm also excited by how experiential the Center's programs are—certificate programs, issue forums, public convenings—because that's where coordination really matters. I'd love to support programs that develop the people leading nonprofits and public institutions, and to do it at an institution like Georgetown that takes social impact seriously. It feels like a place where careful, people-centered program work actually serves a larger purpose.

answers.question: Describe your program coordination experience.
answers.answer: At InGenius Prep I coordinate a portfolio of 20+ educational programs and 10+ vendors end-to-end. Day to day that means owning logistics, scheduling, program materials, and communications—and troubleshooting in real time so disruptions don't reach participants. I run event and webinar logistics, including RSVPs for trainings that reached 600+ participants, which contributed to a 78% enrollment rate. I also build the guides and workflows that keep cross-functional teams aligned, which reduced onboarding time by 75%. Earlier, at Vestas, I directed an M&A integration and standardized onboarding across three global locations. My approach is to diagnose what a program actually needs, build clear systems around it, and stay responsive—my supervisor calls me the most organized person he's worked with.
